methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
General Matter is a Seed-stage nuclear energy company founded by Scott Nolan and Lee Robinson that has raised $50M+. The valuation landscape for nuclear energy comprises 3 public comparables (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

The Stage-Progression method is applicable and forms the basis for valuation. Using total capital raised ($50M) as a proxy for current value and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from Seed to next-stage progression yields $125M. The Peer-Median approach is not applicable; while 8 same-sector private peers exist, the narrow filters (same-stage, adjacent-stage, similar-valuation-tier) do not yield the minimum three comparable datapoints required for reliable median calculation. A sector-wide median would introduce unacceptable noise given the heterogeneity of nuclear energy companies across development phases.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $106M (low) to $144M (high), centered on $125M. This range reflects conservative and optimistic applications of the stage-progression multiplier (2.1x to 2.9x). Key uncertainties include the absence of narrow-set peer comparables, which limits cross-validation of the step-up assumption, and the early-stage nature of the sector, where valuation step-ups may not follow historical patterns. Additional clarity on technology differentiation and capital deployment timeline would refine the range.
